name: Hillary Clinton
subject: Will you sign President Obama’s birthday card, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
So lucky to call him my friend | Peter --

Last week, President Obama gave a beautiful speech at the Democratic convention. He spoke movingly about the challenges we face, the progress we’ve made, and the hope he still has for our country and our future.

I am so lucky to call him my friend. [ ](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=16&ms=MjYxNTAzMzkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYwNjY0NzYyS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Peter, today I have a favor to ask you for President Obama -- his birthday is today, and I want to do something special for him this year to show him how much he means to me and this whole campaign family. **[Will you sign the official campaign birthday card for President Obama so he can see how many people on this team love and appreciate him?](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=14&ms=MjYxNTAzMzkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYwNjY0NzYyS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

I’ve often said that President Obama doesn’t get the credit he deserves for saving our country from the worst economic crisis in decades. Because of his leadership, we have 15 million new jobs, the Affordable Care Act, marriage equality, and so much more.

It means so much to me that he’s a part of this team, and I know he’ll be invaluable as we kick off these final 96 days until the election. So today, let’s show him that we’re grateful for all he’s done for our country, that we’re excited to work with him to win this November, and that we’re committed to working together to protect and build on his remarkable legacy.

**Add your name to sign his card now -- he’ll love knowing that you’re thinking of him on his special day:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjYxNTAzMzkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYwNjY0NzYyS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Hillary

name: John Gregg
subject: No more bridge collapses
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gregg for Indiana
body:
One year ago, the northbound I-65 bridge over Wildcat Creek suddenly dropped 9 inches. But one year later, our state's leaders still refuse to invest the money needed to prevent future collapses.
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/-8738098620889954304/4897804833123600384?utm_campaign=adv_infras_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&refcode=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)
---
Peter, One year ago, the northbound I-65 bridge over Wildcat Creek suddenly dropped 9 inches. It took over a month to repair the damage, as 16,000 cars and 8,000 trucks were forced to find a new route each day. And we still don't know the full cost to Indiana families and businesses. But one year later, our state's leaders still offer no plan to prevent situations like this from happening again. **[It's time to fix our crumbling roads and invest in Indiana's future. Sign my petition if you agree.](https://act.myngp.com/el/-8738098620889954304/4897804833123600384?utm_campaign=adv_infras_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&refcode=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)** Last month, my running mate Christina Hale and I released a comprehensive, fiscally responsible plan to invest $3.2 billion in Indiana's infrastructure over the next 10 years -- without raising taxes. This forward-thinking proposal not only funds repairs and improvements to our roads and bridges -- it also invests dormant state money into updating our outdated water ports, airports, and railroads, expands broadband capacity statewide and in local projects that make communities more livable. Under our plan, we'll create a Hoosier State Infrastructure Bank to fund local needs, enlist experts from private industry to help plan for the future, provide transparency through an open and searchable infrastructure database, return control and resources to local government, and address Indiana's long-term water needs. Together, we can implement this ambitious plan, create an estimated 54,600 new jobs for Hoosiers, and avoid further catastrophes on our roads and bridges. But we need your help to show our state's leaders that it's time for new investment in infrastructure so we can get moving on day one. **[Add your name to tell our state's leaders: "It's time to fix our crumbling roads and invest in Indiana's future!"](https://act.myngp.com/el/-8738098620889954304/4897804833123600384?utm_campaign=adv_infras_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&refcode=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)** Thanks for your support, John
| | [**Add your name ->**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-8738098620889954304/4897804833123600384?utm_campaign=adv_infras_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&refcode=em16160804advinfrasgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)

clean_body: One year ago, the northbound I-65 bridge over Wildcat Creek suddenly dropped 9 inches. But one year later, our state's leaders still refuse to invest the money needed to prevent future collapses. | = Peter, One year ago, the northbound I-65 bridge over Wildcat Creek suddenly dropped 9 inches. It took over a month to repair the damage, as 16,000 cars and 8,000 trucks were forced to find a new route each day. And we still don't know the full cost to Indiana families and businesses. But one year later, our state's leaders still offer no plan to prevent situations like this from happening again. = Last month, my running mate Christina Hale and I released a comprehensive, fiscally responsible plan to invest $3.2 billion in Indiana's infrastructure over the next 10 years -- without raising taxes. This forward-thinking proposal not only funds repairs and improvements to our roads and bridges -- it also invests dormant state money into updating our outdated water ports, airports, and railroads, expands broadband capacity statewide and in local projects that make communities more livable. Under our plan, we'll create a Hoosier State Infrastructure Bank to fund local needs, enlist experts from private industry to help plan for the future, provide transparency through an open and searchable infrastructure database, return control and resources to local government, and address Indiana's long-term water needs. Together, we can implement this ambitious plan, create an estimated 54,600 new jobs for Hoosiers, and avoid further catastrophes on our roads and bridges. But we need your help to show our state's leaders that it's time for new investment in infrastructure so we can get moving on day one. = Thanks for your support, John | | = P.S. Want to read the full infrastructure plan? to take a look. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greg for Montana, PO
body:
Greg for Montana

Governor using your tax dollars for politics

Friend,

Just yesterday, Governor Bullock
again had to pay the state back for using the state plane to fly
to campaign fundraisers. Caught campaigning on the taxpayer
dime!

But his reimbursement was just
pennies on the dollars he owed.

"Gov. Steve Bullock's campaign
has reimbursed the state another $1,155 for extra costs incurred
when pilots who flew him for state business had to wait to return
while he conducted campaign activities."

Legislators say the plane costs
almost $1,650 per hour - and yet the Governor only paid $1,155?

The blatant, continued use of the
state taxpayer funded plane for his political gain and personal
entertainment is insulting. Montana taxpayers should demand a
full refund.

Greg Gianforte won't abuse the
state plane or your taxpayer dollars. He'll dedicate his salary
as Governor to scholarships for veterans and low-income Montana
families.

Let's give Montana a new CEO. Can
you chip in $15, $20 or even $50 today to help the campaign?

Thank you,

Team Greg for Montana

Paid for by Greg for Montana, PO
Box 1557, Helena, MT, 59624, Republican

| **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
August 4, 2016**

**Media Contact:**
Ethan Latour
[ethan@philscott.org](mailto:ethan@philscott.org)
802-595-3776
**Phil Scott Doubles Down on Positive Message with New “High Road” Web Video** **_Scott continues to take the high road, vowing to stay focused on the issues that matter to Vermonters_**
**Middlesex, Vt., August 4, 2016 –** Phil Scott, candidate for governor, today released a web video where he reinforced his commitment to running a positive campaign that’s focused on the issues and sets a good example for our children, a standard he’ll continue to uphold as Governor.

Guided by his conviction that we should treat others as we want to be treated – a conviction that will help restore faith and trust in state government – Scott refuses to join his opponent in the mud, promising he’ll continue to “rise above it.”

“Political experts say I should run slick attack ads against my opponent to combat the dishonest things he’s been saying about me, my plans, and my record,” Scott says in the video. “After nearly two million dollars of his dishonest attacks, I admit, it’s tempting. But we’re not going to do that. We’re going to rise above it. Because I believe you know the truth, and you want campaigns focused on the issues.”

Throughout the campaign, Scott has focused on the issues, detailing his plans to [strengthen the economy, limit spending through clear budget policies](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1433fbf86c&e=19cebfe173), [modernize government and increase efficiency](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d25137e7dd&e=19cebfe173), set priorities to [improve transportation and infrastructure](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c85da8259c&e=19cebfe173), address our [opiate epidemic](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2a0bc2faee&e=19cebfe173), and [more](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=677ed6f1d0&e=19cebfe173). As he vows in his latest web video, he will stay focused on the issues because he knows that’s what Vermonters deserve.

That stance, along with his positions, proposals and record, have earned Scott the support of hundreds of state leaders, and thousands of Vermonters.

“In this primary, I have the support of more than 100 current and former Republican leaders, Governor Jim Douglas, and the Association of Vermont Sheriffs, amongst many others. So if you have any doubts, ask yourself - who do you believe? My opponent? Or Governor Jim Douglas and the tens of thousands of Vermonters who want campaigns and our next Governor to be an example for our children?” Scott concludes.

Learn more about Scott’s principles and read his proposals at [www.philscott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=773582fd4e&e=19cebfe173). Watch the full web video here:
| [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-7ieWGEYJ_4)
---

Lieutenant Governor Phil Scott has been a consistent voice of reason and common sense in Montpelier. As the only Republican elected to statewide office, Phil has shown determination and leadership in advocating for Vermonters and promoting policies and programs that benefit Vermont families and the economy. Read about Phil Scott’s plans at [www.philscott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=58d1c34874&e=19cebfe173).

| Peter --

We're getting ready for a big weekend of volunteering in Woodbridge, and we want to make sure you’ll be there! We’re counting on the support of folks like you to make sure Trump does not get anywhere near the White House, and Democrats win up and down the ballot.

**Will we see you on Saturday? RSVP as soon as you can:**

Hillary for America

\----Forwarded message----
From: Michelle Kleppe, HillaryClinton.com
Subject: Join us on Saturday in Woodbridge to get people pumped up about Hillary!

I don't know about you, Peter, but I'm feeling pretty proud to be a Democrat right now.

At our convention last week, we showed how our party embodies diversity, hope, and the desire to come together to problem-solve -- that's a pretty special thing to be part of. Now it’s time for us to head out into our communities and get our neighbors involved, too.

We're gearing up for a weekend of volunteering, taking all this amazing energy and turning it into real action to help Hillary win this election. **Can you join us for a few hours this weekend to help get people fired up to join Team Hillary?**

Here are the details:
**WHAT:** Hillary for Woodbridge Phone Bank
**WHEN:** Saturday, August 6th
12:00 pm - 06:00 pm
**WHERE:** 4349 Ridgewood Center Dr
Woodbridge, VA 22192

We've got fewer than 50 days before the first votes are cast in this election. It's going to fly by, and we need to do everything we can to make sure that folks in your state know what's at stake, and what a Trump presidency would actually mean.

**So let’s kick into high gear. RSVP now for an event in Woodbridge, and let's go!**

Thanks,

Michelle

Hillary for America

This is truly outrageous --
Control of the Senate and the future of the Supreme Court ride on this race, and right now Governor Hassan is in a dead heat with Kelly Ayotte, who still supports Trump even after his myriad of racist and sexist comments. **Anyone who continues to support Trump for President should be disqualified from holding office.**
If Ayotte won't stand against Trump now, we certainly can't trust that she'll stand against his racist, sexist agenda if they both win this November. **[That's why a group of generous donors has stepped up to match online donations-- but ONLY if we hit our $22,000 goal by tomorrow at midnight. We need you to step up and give every dollar you can to Maggie's campaign. When you do, you could DOUBLE your impact and help us DOUBLE our resources to elect Maggie.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd62b/2a380d31/104e114c/fae250/2190856900/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxNjA4MDJfbWF0Y2giLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE2MDgwNF9NYXRjaDYtVHJ1bXBfbmgtbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/20160802_match?refcode=e20160804_Match6-Trump_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")**
Progressive champions in the Senate, like Elizabeth Warren and Al Franken, have told us that they need Maggie working with them to help build a firewall against the Trump/Ayotte agenda. name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: Donald Trump doesn’t have you, Peter
body:
His numbers are going to get better | Peter --

Yesterday afternoon, I got a push alert on my phone from the _New York Times_. This was the headline:
_"Fueled by Small Donations, Donald Trump Makes Up Major Financial Ground"_
Last month, Donald Trump and the Republican Party raised $82 million and right now, they’re sitting on $74 million in cash on hand.

That’s 90 percent of what we raised. You wouldn't know it by looking at the news, but Trump’s quietly begun to build a real campaign operation. And we can’t expect this to dissipate. In all likelihood, Trump will have a better August than his July and a better September than his August.

That’s unsettling. It could be a real challenge to match his fundraising numbers going forward -- especially because Trump can cut himself a check any time he wants to put his campaign over the top.

But here’s why I’m not panicking: Our campaign hasn’t even begun to reach its full potential.

As of July, 2 million people had stepped up to join our team and make an investment in our work. We’re going to keep growing, keep getting stronger (and smarter) for each of the next 96 days.

Today, I’m asking you to be part of that. Step up, make a donation, and show that we’re clear-eyed about the challenge we face in this election. Prove to the world that we recognize the threat posed by Donald Trump -- and that we’re ready to compete with the energy and intensity of his supporters on any terms this race requires.

**Chip in today -- and when you do, we’ll send you a free sticker to say thanks:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjYxNTgxMzMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYwODU4ODg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks,

Robby

Robby Mook
Campaign Manager
Hillary for America

| **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**
August 4th, 2016

**Media Contact:**
Ethan Latour
[ethan@philscott.org](mailto:ethan@philscott.org)
802-595-3776
**Pro-Lisman PAC Violates State Campaign Finance Law**

_Ads must be taken down as PAC failed to disclose large contributor in ad disclosure_
Montpelier, Vt. – Today the American Future Fund Political Action Committee, an independent expenditure-only PAC, began to air attack ads that are in violation of Vermont’s campaign finance laws, and should be taken off the air.

According to Vermont statute, “an electioneering communication paid for by or on behalf of a political committee or political party shall contain the name of any contributor who contributed more than 25 percent of all contributions and more than $2,000 since the beginning of the two-year election cycle[[1]](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=50bb0a2ec1&e=19cebfe173)”.

The state requires this information to provide transparency around who is paying for political advertising. However, this PAC is violating the law by failing to include that information in the disclaimer of their ad, in clear violation of campaign finance law.

**“We’re not surprised that Bruce Lisman would turn to his Bear Stearns cronies, Robert Steinberg and Warren Spector. Because Robert Steinberg contributed $25,000 to the American Future Fund for the latest media buy, well beyond the 25 percent threshold, his name should be included in the disclaimer. **These last-minute dirty tricks are especially alarming because they were funded by out-of-state interests and show a clear link to Lisman and the Wall Street firm he helped to run that brought America’s economy to its knees,” said Brittney Wilson. “Given the deceitful tactics of the candidate this organization is tied to, it is not surprising that the organization would overlook important transparency provisions which were put in place specifically to make sure Vermonters will not be misled by big-money, out-of-state interests with a hidden agenda,” added Wilson. “The timing is especially ironic as our opponent has been trying to smear Phil’s name over issues Phil himself raised months ago.”

“The ads do not comply with Vermont laws and they must be ordered to be taken down immediately,” Wilson concluded. “They are an illegal and dishonest attempt to mislead Vermonters, and voters deserve better.”

* * *
[1] http://legislature.vermont.gov/statutes/section/17/061/02972

###

Today in Baltimore, vice presidential nominee Tim Kaine vowed that a Clinton-Kaine administration would be a strong partner for America's cities.

Calling himself "an unabashed believer in the power of our cities," Kaine spoke to the National Urban League conference about expanding urban economic opportunity, tackling criminal justice reform, ensuring quality education and leveling the playing field for hardworking families that call cities like Baltimore their home.

You and I know that when cities like Baltimore thrive, surrounding communities and neighborhoods are lifted up as well.

Tomorrow, I'll be speaking to the urban league in Baltimore. Can I tell them you stand with me and Tim Kaine on this? [**Add your name here to pledge your support for aggressive policies that make long term investments in cities like Baltimore possible.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a54/70cdc2ba/47008a22/397695b/2699494417/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-your-name-to-support-baltimore-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+here+to+pledge+your+suppor&utm_campaign=em080416&source=em080416")

Urban revitalization that addresses the needs of everyone in the community requires leadership at all levels of government and partnerships with local business and nonprofit sectors. Working together we can get this done.

Thank you for your support,

Chris

This is our strategy to win. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=OTMwOTA3MQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTgwMzQ5NjY2S0&mt=1&rt=0) | | **Bryant vs. Inslee: It 's Already Getting Heated
\- _Seattle Times_ , 8/3/2016**
---
Dear Friend,
Welcome to the very first few days of the General Election. As the _Seattle Times_ wrote, this race is about to heat up.
Look, **we should all be proud of our primary win, but this race is just beginning.** Here's what's going on:
Bill Bryant's campaign was nearly broke on July 11th before a small handful of mega-donors gave the Republican Party almost $500,000 to buy television ads for Bill Bryant -- giving his campaign a huge boost going into the primary. The Republican Party, backed by these seven mega-donors, bankrolled 90% of our opponent's TV ads. This is outrageous -- it's not how our democracy or our campaigns should work.
[**At this critical moment, Jay needs to count on you. Please rush a donation of $5 to our grassroots campaign today.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=OTMwOTA3MQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTgwMzQ5NjY2S0&mt=2&rj=OTgwMzQ5NjY2S0&rt=0)
We know that this race is a test of our values -- and we know exactly where our opponent stands. That's why we need to keep pushing forward and supporting Jay's campaign, because we know that whether it's Donald Trump's top donor, the RGA, or the Washington State Republican Party, this race is just getting started -- and it's going to be a tough fight.
We know exactly what the next 95 days of this campaign are going to be about -- big money vs. our grassroots campaign. Republican Bill Bryant is relying on special interest donors to carry him through this race. We're running on our record.
[**Help us reach our post-primary fundraising goal of 1,000 donors today. Contribute $5 right now to fight for tolerance and inclusion in Washington state.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=OTMwOTA3MQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTgwMzQ5NjY2S0&mt=2&rj=OTgwMzQ5NjY2S0&rt=0)

And, as someone who starred on "The West Wing," you might say I know a thing or two about politics. That's why I was so excited to get invited to lend a hand in Russ's CheddarBomb today: Because _everyone_ knows that cheese + politics = CheddarBomb! Right now, Russ isn't _just_ up against tea party, Trump-supporting Senator Ron Johnson: He's also up against the ten right-wing groups that have supported Senator Johnson, too. So, to counter these _ten_ outside groups, Russ is trying to get 10,000 grassroots contributions by midnight tonight. **[Simple, right? Today, it's the number of grassroots contributions that counts -- not the sum -- and Russ is inching closer and closer to his goal. It broke my heart when the Koch brothers tried to buy Wisconsin: First, they bought Governor Walker to do their bidding at the capitol. Then, they bought Senator Johnson to do their bidding in Washington.
But I'm so hopeful. Back in 2011, I joined thousands and thousands of my fellow Wisconsinites at our state capitol to protest Governor Walker's attack on workers. That's the moment when I knew we could take Wisconsin back from the Koch brothers, billionaires, and right-wing special interest groups.
Wanna know how I know?
Wisconsin progressives are resilient, and we're not going to go away. This is a constituency that knows patience. _After all, we fish through ice._
**[The window to support Russ's CheddarBomb closes at midnight tonight, and every single dollar gets us closer to reaching 10,000 grassroots contributions to match Senator Ron Johnson's _ten_ right-wing groups. Do what you can right now to blast this CheddarBomb out of the water.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4405/1c469400/78ebcb9/3571a59/1714676644/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-cb?utm_source=ema_2016-08-04_cb_whitford_nd&refcode=ema_2016-08-04_cb_whitford_nd&utm_campaign=cb_whitford&utm_medium=ema")**
Russ is a progressive champion. He listens, he works hard, and you can always count on him to put the wonderful people of Wisconsin first. That's why I'm proud to do all that I can to get him elected.
_(Plus, when he wins, we can all drink from the keg of glory and share the finest muffins and bagels in the land!)_
Thanks, and game on!
Bradley Whitford
P.S. Yes, I know you're wondering: On behalf of fictional President Josiah Bartlet, I hereby formally endorse Russ Feingold for Senate.
